sectionsUnavailable: Authenticated marketplace detail-page extraction beyond the public sample PDF is not available yet.
A calibrated ASCVD or cardiovascular polygenic risk model with clinical and ancestry applicability metadata is not supplied.
LDL cholesterol, ApoB, lipoprotein(a) level, blood pressure, diabetes, smoking, medication, age, sex, family-history context, and personal diet/exercise recommendation context are not supplied.

resultRows.item: Public Cardiovascular Health sample recommendation blocks
resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The public sample PDF shows recommendation-style condition blocks with impact and evidence ratings plus personalized variant tables.
resultRows.description: Sample structure is used to keep the local agent output aligned with the formal report shape; it is not a user-specific diet, supplement, exercise, or treatment recommendation.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: This tells the agent what fields the report can contain, while local genomeEvidence and clinical context determine any user-specific interpretation.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: A derived AG call is available for LPA rs10455872. This is cardiovascular genetics context only and does not measure lipoprotein(a) level.
resultRows.description: Lipoprotein(a) interpretation requires clinical and laboratory context that is not supplied in this fixture.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: This marker can be reported as LPA context, but the report cannot say whether lipoprotein(a) is high without a blood test and clinical review.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: A derived AG call is available for a 9p21 cardiovascular association marker. This marker is not used alone to diagnose heart disease or estimate personal risk.
resultRows.description: The public sample PDF includes impact/evidence style formatting, but this local fixture does not include a calibrated cardiovascular risk model.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Common heart-disease risk is multifactorial, so a single marker cannot determine whether someone has or will develop cardiovascular disease.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: A comprehensive familial hypercholesterolemia pathogenic-variant screen, LDL cholesterol values, family history, physical findings, and clinician diagnosis are unavailable in this fixture.
resultRows.description: The report must mark this unavailable instead of inferring familial hypercholesterolemia from the supplied rows.
resultRows.sourceLabel: CDC About Familial Hypercholesterolemia; MedlinePlus familial hypercholesterolemia
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: This local package cannot diagnose familial hypercholesterolemia or rule it out because the required clinical and variant-screening evidence is not present.

resultRows.item: Calibrated cardiovascular risk or recommendation model
resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The fixture does not include a calibrated ASCVD or cardiovascular polygenic risk model, ancestry applicability metadata, clinical risk factors, or personal diet/exercise/supplement recommendation context.
resultRows.description: Public sample recommendation blocks can be mirrored as format only; they do not authorize personal recommendations from this fixture.
resultRows.sourceLabel: NHGRI overview of polygenic risk scores; MedlinePlus complex disorders
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: The report can summarize supplied genetic context, but it cannot estimate personal heart attack, stroke, cholesterol, ApoB, or ASCVD risk or recommend a diet/exercise plan from this fixture.

sampleRows.description: The public sample block discusses avoiding trans fats, limiting saturated fats, and replacing them with healthier unsaturated fats for triglyceride context.
sampleRows.sourceBindingNote: Extracted from the public Cardiovascular Health sample PDF recommendation block; this is observed output structure, not personal advice.

sampleRows.description: The public sample block says consuming unsaturated fats is associated with lower ApoB levels.
sampleRows.sourceBindingNote: Extracted from the public Cardiovascular Health sample PDF recommendation block; this is observed output structure, not personal advice.

sampleRows.description: The public sample block includes a personalized APOE rs429358 TT variant row and says healthy fats may increase LDL particle size more in people with that sample variant.
sampleRows.sourceBindingNote: Extracted from the public Cardiovascular Health sample PDF recommendation and personalized-variant block.

sampleRows.description: The public sample block includes a heart-rate-variability condition row in the healthy-fats recommendation excerpt.
sampleRows.sourceBindingNote: Extracted from the public Cardiovascular Health sample PDF recommendation block; this is observed output structure, not personal advice.

sampleRows.description: The public sample block discusses fiber for cholesterol context and names a personalized CYP7A1 rs3808607 GT variant in the surrounding prose/table.
sampleRows.sourceBindingNote: Extracted from the public Cardiovascular Health sample PDF; the table gene cell OCR is noisy, but the sample prose names CYP7A1.

sampleRows.description: The public sample block discusses fiber for triglyceride context and names a personalized FABP2 rs1799883 cc variant in the surrounding prose/table.
sampleRows.sourceBindingNote: Extracted from the public Cardiovascular Health sample PDF; the table gene cell OCR is noisy, but the sample prose names FABP2.

sampleRows.description: The public sample block says a high intake of dietary fiber is associated with lower ApoB levels.
sampleRows.sourceBindingNote: Extracted from the public Cardiovascular Health sample PDF recommendation block; this is observed output structure, not personal advice.

sampleRows.description: The public sample block includes a hemorrhoids condition row inside the cardiovascular sample recommendation excerpt.
sampleRows.sourceBindingNote: Extracted from the public Cardiovascular Health sample PDF recommendation block; this is observed output structure, not personal advice.

riskContext.plainEnglishMeaning: Cardiovascular health depends on genetics plus LDL cholesterol, ApoB, lipoprotein(a) levels, blood pressure, diabetes, smoking, age, sex, family history, ancestry, medications, lifestyle, environment, and clinical care.
unsupportedClaims: high cholesterol, high ApoB, or high lipoprotein(a) diagnosis
riskContext.professionalReview: A clinician can interpret cardiovascular genetics together with lipid labs, blood pressure, family history, symptoms, medications, age, sex, ancestry, and other health information.

references.title: NHGRI overview of polygenic risk scores

genotypeSummary.observedValue: public sample variant-table shape only; no local user call supplied for these sample recommendation rows
pathogenic-variant screen, LDL cholesterol values, family history, physical findings, and clinician diagnosis unavailable

missingInputs: Authenticated marketplace detail-page sections beyond the public sample PDF have not been extracted.

uncertainty: No calibrated probability model is supplied for this local package.
The observed marker rows are context only and cannot estimate a personal cardiovascular event risk.
The public sample PDF demonstrates output shape and example recommendation rows, not this user's personalized cardiovascular recommendations.
limitations: This validation result uses derived evidence only; raw genome data is not included.
The package cannot diagnose familial hypercholesterolemia, coronary artery disease, high cholesterol, high ApoB, high lipoprotein(a), heart attack, stroke, or aortic valve disease.
The package does not recommend medication, supplements, procedures, lab tests, imaging, genetic testing decisions, diet changes, exercise plans, or treatment changes.
Sample impact/evidence ratings and recommendation blocks are observed report structure only unless separate calibrated recommendation evidence is supplied.
